feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Severe Thunderstorm Watch
entry.summary: A front and a trough with heavy rain and embedded thunderstorms are expected to move east over the northern North Island this evening. The thunderstorms will be accompanied by heavy rain of 10-25mm/h, strong wind gust of 100km/h, and a low risk of a small tornado. For Coromandel Peninsula, there is also a moderate risk of localised downpours of 25-40mm/h between about 7pm and 10pm today (Friday). These downpours could occur with or without thunderstorms. Rainfall of this intensity can cause surface and/or flash flooding, especially about low-lying areas such as streams, rivers or narrow valleys, and may also lead to slips. Driving conditions will also be hazardous with surface flooding and poor visibility in heavy rain.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Severe Thunderstorm Watch
entry.summary: A front and a trough with heavy rain and embedded thunderstorms are expected to move east over the northern North Island this evening. The thunderstorms will be accompanied by heavy rain of 10-25mm/h, strong wind gust of 100km/h, and a low risk of a small tornado. For Northland, Auckland (including Great Barrier Island) and Waikato, there is also a moderate risk of localised downpours of 25-40mm/h between about 7pm and 9pm today (Friday). These downpours could occur with or without thunderstorms. Rainfall of this intensity can cause surface and/or flash flooding, especially about low-lying areas such as streams, rivers or narrow valleys, and may also lead to slips. Driving conditions will also be hazardous with surface flooding and poor visibility in heavy rain.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect a further 60 to 80 mm of rain on top of what has already fallen. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h, but possibly 25 to 40 mm/h in localised downpours. Squally thunderstorms possible. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect a further 40 to 70 mm of rain on top of what has already fallen. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h, especially in thunderstorms. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain with possible thunderstorms, and amounts may approach warning criteria. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Tararua Range has been upgraded to an Orange Warning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, and amounts may approach warning criteria. Thunderstorms possible. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect a further 20 to 40mm on top of what has already fallen, but 40 to 60mm about Taranaki Maunga. Peak rates of 15 to 25mm/h, with thunderstorms possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal. Changes: Note, warning extended for a further three hours.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, and amounts may approach warning criteria. Thunderstorms possible. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
